pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: NEVER INTRATHECAL - FATAL if given by this route. Must be dispensed in a minibag.
mandatory_checks: Verify patient ID with two identifiers

extravasation_management.action: Stop infusion immediately, do not remove needle. Notify physician stat.
extravasation_management.antidote: Hyaluronidase 150 units injected locally in 5 divided sites around extravasation area

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Continuous observation during infusion; monitor vitals every 15 minutes
stop_criteria: Patient reports burning, stinging, or pain at IV site
Redness, swelling, or blanching at IV site

nursing_assessment: Check IV site for signs of extravasation every 15 min for 1 hour post-infusion
Monitor fluid intake and urine output
Monitor for delayed hypersensitivity reactions (24-48 hours)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: Drink at least 3 liters of fluid daily unless contraindicated. Report any signs of infection, bleeding, or unusual bruising to your healthcare provider immediately. Avoid NSAIDs.

manage_at_bedside: Mild nausea: provide antiemetic per protocol

pre_infusion_safety.fatal_alerts: Prehydration mandatory. Renal dysfunction and ototoxicity risk. Check baseline creatinine <1.5x normal.

extravasation_management.action: Stop infusion immediately. Apply ice to area. Notify physician.
extravasation_management.antidote: Sodium thiosulfate 1/6 M injected into extravasation site

during_infusion.monitoring_frequency: Monitor vitals every 15 minutes during infusion; strict I&O

nursing_assessment: Monitor urine output (goal >200mL/hr for 4 hours post-infusion)
Monitor for ototoxicity (ask about hearing changes)
post_infusion_care.patient_education: You may experience some ringing in your ears or temporary hearing changes. Report this immediately. Maintain good fluid intake. Some hair loss is expected.

manage_at_bedside: Mild electrolyte changes: ensure adequate hydration
